div1.entryFree: Troop, vb. to march in a body, to march in company: I second thee; t. on, Wiv. I, 3, 114 (Pistol's speech). at whose approach ghosts . . . t. home to churchyards, Mids. III, 2, 382. nor do I as an enemy to peace t. in the throngs of military men, H4B IV, 1, 62. there will the lovely Roman ladies t. Tit. II, 1, 113. a snowy dove --ing with crows, Rom. I, 5, 50. all the large effects that t. with majesty, Lr. I, 1, 134.
